title: Rustic Peruvian Ladyfingers Fritters
description: A dairy-free, briny and crumbly twist on picarones, using Savoiardi ladyfingers folded into a spiced batter and pan-fried to golden perfection.
instructions: Preheat a wok over medium heat and pour 200 ml vegetable oil, allowing it to heat until shimmering but not smoking.
In a bowl, whisk together tapioca flour, potato starch, sugar, baking soda, salt, cumin, coriander and chili powder until well combined.
Gradually pour in coconut milk while whisking to form a smooth batter that resembles a thick pancake batter.
Cut each ladyfinger into quarters and add them to the batter, letting them soak for 30 seconds.
Stir the soaked ladyfingers into the batter until each piece is fully coated.
Add the chopped cilantro and a tablespoon of lemon juice, mixing gently.
Using a spoon, drop spoonfuls of the batter into the hot oil, arranging them in a single layer.
Fry the fritters for 2-3 minutes per side or until golden brown and crisp.
Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Serve hot, dusted with a pinch of sea salt and a squeeze of lime if desired.
